claim: MCP's initial authentication spec (March 2024) combined authentication and resource servers, limiting enterprise integration with centralized identity providers; separating these roles in June spec enabled proper OAuth-style architecture supporting IDP delegation
actionable: When designing authentication for agent protocols, separate the token provider from the resource server to support enterprise identity federation patterns
source_title: One Year of MCP — with David Soria Parria and AAIF leads

claim: Streamable HTTP in MCP allows optional bidirectional streams for clients, but since the feature is optional, most implementations omit it, eliminating elicitations and sampling features that require bidirectional communication
actionable: When designing protocols with optional features, recognize that making features optional often results in minimal client implementations; consider making critical bidirectional features mandatory or providing a simpler opt-in path
source_title: One Year of MCP — with David Soria Parria and AAIF leads

claim: Streamable HTTP requires holding state on the server side; horizontally scaled servers (multiple pods) need shared state (Redis/Memcache) to correlate tool calls with elicitation results across different server instances, becoming a scaling bottleneck for million-request-scale deployments
actionable: Evaluate whether your protocol design requires server-side state correlation across requests; if yes, plan for distributed state management early or redesign to be stateless
source_title: One Year of MCP — with David Soria Parria and AAIF leads

claim: Progressive discovery (asking model to selectively retrieve tool details) is a general principle any tool-calling model can apply; model post-training optimizes this behavior but doesn't enable it—models already possess this capability in principle
actionable: Design prompts and tool specifications assuming models can discover and request additional context progressively rather than loading all tools upfront
source_title: One Year of MCP — with David Soria Parria and AAIF leads

claim: MCP (horizontal communication layer for external actions) and Skills (vertical domain knowledge) are orthogonal: Skills provide task-specific behavior patterns; MCP provides actionable connectivity; combined deployment has Skills select actions and MCP servers execute them
actionable: Use Skills to define how models should behave in specific domains; use MCP to safely connect to external data and services; combine them by having Skills orchestrate MCP server calls
source_title: One Year of MCP — with David Soria Parria and AAIF leads

claim: Programmatic MCP (code mode) is a token optimization: instead of tool-call → inference → tool-call chaining, models compose sequential MCP calls into code executed in sandbox; MCP's authentication, discovery, and self-documentation benefits remain unchanged
actionable: Consider code generation over tool chains when: token efficiency matters, models are strong at code, execution can be sandboxed; recognize this as optimization, not a replacement for MCP value
source_title: One Year of MCP — with David Soria Parria and AAIF leads

claim: Public registries (like npm) suffer from supply-chain attacks and trust issues; the solution is curated sub-registries: official registry + filtered sub-registries (e.g., GitHub-curated, company-internal) all speaking same API, allowing agents to auto-select servers
actionable: Implement multi-tier registry architecture: public (low trust) + curated sub-registries (high trust) with standardized discovery API for agent auto-selection
source_title: One Year of MCP — with David Soria Parria and AAIF leads

claim: For long-running agent tasks with many back-and-forth messages, models naturally drop unnecessary tool results; future optimization involves training models to selectively retain context or using smaller models as filters ('what should I retain?')
actionable: For multi-round agent tasks, implement context pruning: drop tool results models don't need; optionally use auxiliary model calls to filter context retention
source_title: One Year of MCP — with David Soria Parria and AAIF leads

claim: MCP Tasks primitive encapsulates long-running operations; design allows future support for intermediate result reporting and full observability; polling-based core interface (client pulls until done) can be optimized with webhooks or event streams
actionable: Use Tasks for operations exceeding single inference roundtrip; implement polling initially, add webhook callbacks for scale optimization
source_title: One Year of MCP — with David Soria Parria and AAIF leads

claim: Financial services impose legal attribution requirements: when exposing third-party data, MCP servers must guarantee attribution through client behavior; enforcement may require certifications (e.g., 'financial services MCP client') and server-side access control
actionable: In regulated industries, design MCP servers and clients with explicit attribution enforcement; require client capability proof (signatures) before granting access to regulated data
source_title: One Year of MCP — with David Soria Parria and AAIF leads

claim: Multi-server agent sessions with regulated data require session-level isolation: a HIPAA-protected MCP server can enforce that other servers in the session cannot receive patient data; this is a client-side and protocol-level concern, not just auth
actionable: When building agentic systems with sensitive data, use MCP servers that declare data classification; enforce server isolation rules at the client level (disable other MCP servers during sensitive data access)
source_title: One Year of MCP — with David Soria Parria and AAIF leads

claim: Enterprise adoption of MCP is largely invisible (internal tool integrations); leading companies deploy MCP at millions-of-requests scale with custom gateways (Anthropic, Google, Microsoft) hiding protocol complexity from end users
actionable: When deploying MCP in enterprise settings, build an internal gateway/orchestration layer (custom or using LiteLLM-style proxies) to simplify server management and add auth/audit layers
source_title: One Year of MCP — with David Soria Parria and AAIF leads

claim: MCP Apps (UI components served as HTML/iframe over MCP resource protocol) extend MCP beyond data/tools to rich UI interactions; iframes provide natural security boundaries and support agent-native UI navigation
actionable: For complex integrations (flight booking, shopping), serve interactive HTML UIs via MCP as resources; use post-message API for agent-UI communication within iframe security boundaries
source_title: One Year of MCP — with David Soria Parria and AAIF leads

claim: MCP Apps served in iframes cannot inherit parent styles; branding consistency across ChatGPT, Claude, Goose requires either inline styles in HTML or client capability to pass style definitions; long-term solution unclear
actionable: When building MCP Apps, inline all styles and avoid external CSS dependencies; coordinate with client developers on brand integration approach
source_title: One Year of MCP — with David Soria Parria and AAIF leads

claim: Gateway solutions (Fast MCP, LiteLLM) convert standard-IO MCP servers to remote (StreamHTTP) with automatic authentication and orchestration; two-command deployment pattern reduces operational overhead
actionable: Evaluate LiteLLM or Fast MCP for wrapping existing stdio-based MCP servers; reduces DevOps complexity for remote deployment
source_title: One Year of MCP — with David Soria Parria and AAIF leads

claim: Sampling (conditional tool calling) was intended for local MCP use; remote deployments better served by SDK-based implementations with full control; most MCP servers now omit sampling support
actionable: For sampling-heavy workflows, prefer SDK implementations over remote MCP servers; if using remote MCP, implement sampling logic client-side or via separate API
source_title: One Year of MCP — with David Soria Parria and AAIF leads

claim: Majority of MCP usage is read-only: contextualization with data; action-oriented workflows (updating Linear, etc.) are common but less frequent than data retrieval tasks
actionable: Optimize MCP servers for data read performance; implement action workflows via Tasks when write operations dominate
source_title: One Year of MCP — with David Soria Parria and AAIF leads

claim: Emerging high-value MCP use case: long-running deep research (hours-long agent loops with multiple stages) leveraging new Tasks primitive; uncommon currently but expected to grow post-SDK rollout
actionable: For research-heavy applications, design MCP servers to expose research workflow stages as Tasks with observable progress and intermediate results
source_title: One Year of MCP — with David Soria Parria and AAIF leads

claim: Agentic AI Foundation separates technical governance (core maintainers decide) from funding/membership governance; prevents pay-to-play influence on protocol decisions while allowing resource allocation to community benefit
actionable: When structuring open-source foundations, decouple technical decisions (via small core maintainer group) from funding allocation (via broader board); reduces vendor capture while enabling shared resources
source_title: One Year of MCP — with David Soria Parria and AAIF leads

claim: Protocol maturity requires feedback from multiple implementations (Goose, Claude, ChatGPT); testing a standard against one client/server pair leaves gaps; foundation approach enables concrete validation before spec finalization
actionable: When designing protocols for multi-implementer ecosystems, establish reference implementations early; require protocol changes to be validated across at least 2 independent implementations
source_title: One Year of MCP — with David Soria Parria and AAIF leads
